what_it_does: Defines the Workshop block renderer registry on window.WS_BLOCKS, including default data and React render functions for editable content blocks, visual/layout blocks, diagrams, metrics, embeds, and utility helpers used by those blocks.
observations: Uses global React and browser globals rather than imports or exports.
Edit wraps contentEditable text, substitutes workspace variables for display, and commits changed text on blur.
Many renderers update nested block data by shallow-copying arrays or objects before calling set.
Several icon-based blocks render SVG content from window.CV_ICONS.data through dangerouslySetInnerHTML.
Cross-doc embed blocks read workspace documents through window.useWSDocs and provide picker or live preview states.
standouts: The file is a large declarative block catalog where each block carries its label, icon, defaults, and renderer together.
The editable text wrapper also activates a global per-field regeneration toolbar using DOM position and section metadata.
Widget and wizard embed blocks make other Workshop documents visible inside canvas content.
conventions: Block definitions are keyed by data.kind values inside a single window.WS_BLOCKS object.
Render functions generally accept data and set, then call set with a full updated block data object.
Editable copy is consistently routed through the Edit component.
CSS class names use the ws-blk-* prefix for block presentation hooks.
The file relies on optional global services with guarded window checks.
concerns: contentEditable is combined with dangerouslySetInnerHTML, so displayed substituted content and stored textContent can diverge when variables are present.
Some nested updates assume arrays such as data.items, data.steps, data.lines, or data.rows exist when editing existing rendered entries.
The block registry is monolithic, mixing many unrelated block families and helper components in one file.
Icon SVG injection trusts window.CV_ICONS.data content.
The Edit component uses direct DOM inspection and global toolbar coupling, making behavior dependent on surrounding markup and globals.
notes: This file is the Workshop canvas block catalog plus the shared inline editing and embed helpers that make those blocks interactive.
questions: How are invalid or missing block data shapes normalized before these renderers receive data?
What owns the lifecycle and positioning of window.WS_FIELD outside this file?
Are workspace variable substitutions intended to be editable as rendered text or as their original template strings?
purpose_vs_actual: The header says the file contains block renderers, and the file does that while also including shared editing, color, and cross-document embed helper logic.
intention_signals: It appears intended to let a canvas/workshop editor render many block types from structured data while keeping text directly editable in place and allowing reusable widgets or wizards to be embedded live.
novelty: The distinct aspect is the combination of a broad block palette, inline editable fields, per-field regeneration activation, and live cross-document embed previews in one browser-global renderer file.
contribution: This file contributes a self-contained ledger of Workshop block substrates: editable typography, metrics, process diagrams, icon/tag systems, organization diagrams, contact and bio cards, timelines, and live widget/wizard embeds, all expressed as structured default data plus render functions.
summary_for_embedding: A React/browser-global Workshop block library that maps block kinds to labels, icons, defaults, and renderers, with inline editable text, color luminance support, and live widget/wizard embed components.
intention_for_embedding: It was for rendering and editing structured design/content blocks inside a Workshop canvas while supporting workspace variables, field-level generation tooling, and cross-document previews.

symbols.does: Renders inline editable text and commits text changes through onChange on blur.
symbols.description: Edit is a reusable contentEditable wrapper used across block renderers. It displays workspace-variable-substituted content when available, activates the global field toolbar on focus or key/mouse interaction, stores field metadata through data-ws-field, and delays toolbar deactivation after blur.

symbols.does: Activates window.WS_FIELD for the current editable field using DOM position, section metadata, and an apply callback.
symbols.description: activate is defined inside Edit and reads the editable element rectangle plus nearest data-section-id context. It passes value, block kind, section id, inferred or explicit field name, and an onApply handler into the global field toolbar service.

symbols.does: Infers a field name from an element class matching ws-blk-* patterns.
symbols.description: guessFieldName inspects the element className and extracts the suffix from the first ws-blk- prefixed class. It returns an empty string when no matching class hint is found.

symbols.does: Determines whether a hex color is light using a weighted RGB luminance threshold.
symbols.description: isLight accepts short or full hex color strings, expands short hex form, parses RGB channels, and compares weighted brightness against 175. It is assigned to window.isLight and used by the palette block to choose contrasting text color.

symbols.does: Renders a widget embed picker or a live embedded widget preview from workspace documents.
symbols.description: EmbedWidget subscribes to workspace docs through window.useWSDocs, filters for docs with type widget, and resolves the selected docId. Without a target it shows an empty state and picker; with a target it renders window.WidgetRender and metadata with a Change button.

symbols.does: Renders a wizard embed picker or a compact live wizard step preview from workspace documents.
symbols.description: EmbedWizard subscribes to workspace docs through window.useWSDocs, filters for docs with type wizard, and resolves the selected docId. Without a target it shows an empty state and picker; with a target it displays wizard title, kind, step count, a Change button, and up to six step summaries.
